criteria: The agent has read Section 2.2.1 completely to understand the ARIMA model components (AR, I, MA) and the differencing operation.
The agent has read Section 2.2.2 completely to understand the Transit Comb Filter (TCF) periodogram and its role in identifying double-spike patterns in ARIMA residuals.

The agent has read Section 2.4 to understand the DIAmante light curve input: 976,814 stars from TESS Year 1 sectors 1-13, restricted to FGK stars with V<13 and M stars with V<=16 and D<600pc, and log g >= 3.
The agent has read Section 2.5 to understand the additional ramping and outlier removal step applied to DIAmante light curves before ARIMA fitting.
The agent has read Section 4.1 completely to understand the Kepler-based planet injection methodology for the positive training set.
The agent has read Section 4.2 to understand the negative training set construction with injected eclipsing binary and sinusoidal variable signals.
The agent has read Table 1 to understand all 37 features and their weights used in the final Random Forest classifier.
The agent has read Table 2 to understand the classification metrics (TPR, FPR, MCC, Adjusted F-Score, Youden's J) for the chosen threshold PRF=0.300.
The agent has read Table 3 and Figure 13 to understand the DTARPS-S Analysis List of 7,377 objects and the RF prediction distribution.
The agent has read Section 9 completely to understand the completeness analysis of the RF classifier on injected planetary signals.

The plan includes the preprocessing pipeline: loading DIAmante light curves and applying the additional ramping/outlier removal step with outlier threshold of 5 sigma and gap threshold of 50 time steps.
The plan includes the ARIMA modeling step: applying differencing (d=1) first, then fitting ARMA(p,q) models with p+q <= 10 using auto.arima with AIC model selection.
The plan includes running the TCF periodogram on ARIMA residuals over periods 0.2 to 30 days (354,982 periods in log-space), with LOESS detrending of the periodogram.
The plan includes the ARIMAX model fitting using the best TCF period to jointly fit transit depth and autocorrelated noise.

The plan includes constructing the positive training set: downloading 949 Kepler confirmed planets with P<13.5 days and Kepler Model.SNR>=20, augmenting to 10,850 synthetic planets using ANS-SMOTE, injecting into 6,506 random DIAmante light curves, and recovering 1,327 via TCF human vetting.

The plan includes constructing the negative training set: 11,342 injected False Positive light curves (EBs in circular/eccentric orbits and sinusoidal variables in equal thirds) plus 15,611 random light curves.
The plan includes splitting the labeled data 80/20 into training and validation sets (training: 1,048 positive, 9,095 FP, 12,475 random; validation: 279 positive, 2,247 FP, 3,136 random).
The plan includes the Random Forest classifier optimization: balanced RF with 1,000 trees, 37 features with weights, threshold PRF=0.300, using CRAN randomForestSRC.
The plan includes applying the final RF classifier to 823,099 DIAmante light curves and producing the DTARPS-S Analysis List of 7,377 objects.
The plan includes generating the completeness heat map of RF recall rates as a function of injected period and radius bins from 7,751 synthetic planet injections.
The plan includes computing the effective SNR (SNR^eff) for injected planetary signals using Equation 8 and analyzing the lower bound of SNR^eff=6 for recovered injections.

The plan includes comparing DTARPS-S recall rates against M20 DIAmante candidates (364 processed), NASA Exoplanet Archive confirmed planets (202 in DIAmante), TOI list (846 overlap), and previously identified False Positives (513 in DIAmante).
The code for loading and preprocessing DIAmante light curves has been implemented, including reading the extracted light curve files from Montalto (2020b).
The code for the additional ramping and outlier removal step is implemented: recursive clipping with outlier threshold 5*sigma from the median and gap threshold of 50 time steps (25 hours).
The code for the differencing operation (Equation 2) is implemented: x_t^dif = x_t - x_{t-1}, applied to the light curve before ARMA fitting to guarantee the double-spike transit pattern.
The code for the AR(p) component (Equation 3) is implemented: x_t = phi_1*x_{t-1} + ... + phi_p*x_{t-p} + epsilon_t, where epsilon ~ N(0, sigma^2).
The code for the MA(q) component (Equation 4) is implemented: x_t = epsilon_t + theta_1*epsilon_{t-1} + ... + theta_q*epsilon_{t-q}.
The ARIMA model fitting is implemented using the auto.arima function from the R forecast package with the constraint p+q <= 10 and d=1, with AIC-based model selection.
The Ljung-Box test is applied to both original light curves and ARIMA residuals to quantify autocorrelation, with p-value > 0.01 indicating white noise.
The TCF (Transit Comb Filter) periodogram Fortran code is integrated and called from the R-based DTARPS pipeline, searching periods 0.2 to 30 days using 354,982 log-spaced trial periods.
The TCF periodogram implementation searches transit durations from 15% of the period to a maximum of 25 hours (50 time steps) for each trial period.
The LOESS smoothing is applied to the TCF periodogram to remove trend, and the best TCF peak SNR is computed within a window of 10,000 periodogram values around each peak.
The ARIMAX model is implemented using auto.arima with the transit mask as an exogenous variable, fitting transit depth with confidence interval from the best TCF period/phase/duration.
The stellar selection cuts for planet injection hosts are implemented: temperature/radius-dependent subgiant exclusion criteria (e.g., T_eff < 4750K and R < 1 R_sun, etc.).
The code to download and filter the 949 Kepler confirmed planets from the NASA Exoplanet Archive (P<13.5 days, Kepler Model.SNR>=20) for use as injection templates is implemented.

The ANS-SMOTE (Adaptive Neighbor SMOTE) algorithm is implemented using the smotefamily R package to augment the 949 Kepler planets to 10,850 synthetic exoplanets with matching period/duration/depth distributions.

The transit injection code is implemented: a trapezoidal-shaped transit model with period, depth, duration, and random phase is added point-wise to DIAmante light curves after preprocessing but before DTARPS-S processing.

The human vetting criterion for recovered injections is implemented: TCF period matches injected period (or integer ratio) within 1%, and a transit dip is visible in the folded light curve, yielding 1,327 of 10,850 recovered injections.

The negative training set injected False Positives are implemented: EB signals with secondaries > 2.5 R_Jupiter in circular and eccentric orbits, plus short-period sinusoidal variables mimicking spotted stars, in equal thirds (11,342 total FP injections).

The 80/20 random train/validation split is implemented on the full labeled dataset: 22,618 training objects (1,048 positive, 9,095 FP, 12,475 random) and 5,662 validation objects (279 positive, 2,247 FP, 3,136 random).

All 37 features listed in Table 1 are computed for each light curve, including stellar properties (TIC v8 and Gaia DR2), DIAmante light curve properties (reduced chi^2, tail range, POM, skewness, kurtosis, Ljung-Box p-value), differenced light curve properties, ARIMA residual properties, TCF periodogram properties, and best TCF transit properties.
The IQR improvement ratio (IQR of ARIMA residuals / IQR of DIAmante light curve) and reduced chi^2 improvement ratio features are implemented with high feature weight (0.108) as specified in Table 1.

The eleven best TCF transit features are implemented with weight 0.135 each: TCF_power, TCF_depthSNR, TCF_shape (mean in-transit / MAD out-of-transit), Folded_AD (Anderson-Darling test on phases), arbox_deperr, even.odd.p_value, trans.p_value, snr.transit, frac_dur, LOESS_mnsnr, TCFpeaks_mean, LOESSpeaks_mean.
The planet radius feature (planet_rad_tcf) is computed from the TCF transit depth and the TIC stellar radius, included to help the RF classifier reject deep eclipsing binary signals.
The Matthews Correlation Coefficient (MCC, Equation 5), Adjusted F-Score (Equation 6), and Youden's J Index (Equation 7) are implemented for classifier evaluation.
The ROC curve and Precision-Recall curve are generated using the ROCR R package, with AUC computed for both curves during classifier optimization.
The balanced Random Forest classifier is implemented using randomForestSRC R package with balanced RF option (undersampling majority class per tree), 1,000 trees, and 7 features tried at each node.

The RF classifier optimization loop is implemented: training approximately 20,000 trial classifiers (500-1000 trees each), filtering by AUC-ROC > 0.9 and AUC-PR > 0.85, and iterating feature selection/weight tuning over 4+ rounds.
The 133 random M20 DIAmante candidates are included in the final optimization round with their TPR used as an additional criterion for final classifier selection.
The out-of-bag (OOB) prediction values are used for training set performance evaluation in the confusion matrix, with the chosen threshold PRF=0.300 applied.
The effective SNR formula (Equation 8: SNR^eff = (delta/IQR) * sqrt(n_pts * T_dur/P)) is implemented using IQR instead of standard deviation for both injected signal and best TCF transit assessment.
The completeness heat map is implemented: computing recall rate for each log-spaced period-radius bin from 7,751 synthetic planet injections processed by the RF classifier at threshold PRF=0.300.
The period matching logic is implemented for comparing TCF periods to external catalog periods: match within 1% fractional difference or matching an integer harmonic ratio of the reported period.
Cross-matching of DIAmante light curves with external catalogs (NASA Exoplanet Archive, TOI list, M20, and other surveys) is implemented using TIC ID and/or 5 arcsecond coordinate matching.
The full ARIMA modeling pipeline has been executed on the ~0.9 million DIAmante light curves without errors, producing ARIMA residuals for all stars.
The TCF periodogram has been run on ARIMA residuals for all processed light curves, producing periodograms with best TCF period, depth, and SNR for each star.
The 10,850 planet injections (and all FP injections) have been processed through the full DTARPS-S pipeline (preprocessing, ARIMA, TCF) without errors.
The Random Forest classifier optimization has been executed, training approximately 20,000 trial classifiers across multiple optimization rounds using the R randomForestSRC package.
The final RF classifier with 37 features, 1,000 trees, and threshold PRF=0.300 has been applied to the 823,099 DIAmante light curves without errors, producing the DTARPS-S Analysis List.
The completeness heat map analysis has been executed on 7,751 synthetic planet injections processed through the RF classifier, producing recall rates by period-radius bins.
The Ljung-Box test results match Figure 7: 46% of DIAmante light curves have significant autocorrelation (p<0.01) before ARIMA modeling, and only 4% after ARIMA residual modeling.
The injection recovery statistics match Section 4.1: of 10,850 synthetic planetary injections, 1,327 (approximately 12%) are recovered by TCF and human vetting for use as the positive training set.
The final RF classifier performance matches Table 2 at threshold PRF=0.300: TPR=92.83% (approximately 92.5%), FPR=0.37% (approximately 0.43%), MCC=0.9246, Adjusted F-Score=0.9283, Youden's J=0.9246.
The confusion matrix matches Figure 12: only 2 out of 11,342 (0.02%) injected FP signals are falsely labeled as exoplanet transit signals in the labeled dataset.
The RF classifier applied to 823,099 DIAmante light curves produces a DTARPS-S Analysis List of 7,377 objects (0.9% of input), matching the reported result.

The period distribution of the DTARPS-S Analysis List matches Figure 13: the vast majority of injected planets are recovered with periods ~0.7 to 11 days, with a strong spike of false negatives at 13-15 day periods due to the TESS satellite orbital period.

The TCF radius bias matches Section 8.1: recovered injected planets show TCF radii slightly below the 1:1 line, with more pronounced bias for radii > 10 R_earth; 43% of unrecovered injections with R<4 R_earth have TCF radii more than twice the true radius.

The completeness heat map matches Figure 17: poor completeness (<10%) for radii < 2 R_earth across all periods; low completeness (10%-25%) for 2-4 R_earth; high completeness (70%-100%) for radii 8-30 R_earth and periods 0.6-13 days.
The SNR^eff analysis matches Figure 18: approximately 93% of rejected injected planetary signals lie below the SNR^eff=6 boundary; recovery rate above SNR^eff=6 is approximately 71%.

The M20 DIAmante candidate recall rate matches Section 10.1: 213 of 364 processed M20 candidates (59%) are in the DTARPS-S Analysis List, with TCF periods matching M20 periods in 333 of 364 cases (91%).

The NASA Exoplanet Archive confirmed planet recall rate matches Section 10.2: 130 of 202 confirmed planets (64%) are recovered in the DTARPS-S Analysis List, with TCF correctly matching periods for 166 of 202 (82%) confirmed planets.
The TOI recall rate matches Section 10.3: 433 of 846 TOIs (51%) are in the DTARPS-S Analysis List, with TCF matching periods for 566 of 706 non-long-period TOIs (80%).

The False Positive specificity matches Section 10.5: 400 of 513 (78%) previously identified False Positives are correctly classified as non-exoplanet candidates by the RF classifier, with 90% specificity for periods <1 day and 97% for periods >10 days.
The feature importance ranking matches Figure 14: snr.transit is the most important feature, followed by arbox_deperr, TCF_period, and planet_rad_tcf among the top features.
